checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?
Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?
Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?

evidence.location: 10-K FY2009 selected financial data / cash flow statement

checklist.question: Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: Revenues=9600564000 (2008); Revenues=8826933000 (2009) - an 8.1% decline while inventory rose 7.3%
CostOfRevenue=7622015000 (2008); CostOfRevenue=8123078000 (2009) - gross margin compressed sharply even as inventory grew

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss=164802000 (Q1 2009); NetIncomeLoss=41981000 (Q2 2009); NetIncomeLoss=77374000 (Q3 2009) - substantial quarter-to-quarter swings consistent with revenue/margin volatility

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: ReceivablesNetCurrent=2442848000 (2008-12-31) growing to ReceivablesNetCurrent=2535625000 (2010-09-30) - allowance grew faster in percentage terms than receivables (ratio rose from ~0.67% to ~0.97%)

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: form=10-K/A filing_date=2010-04-30 (amending the FY2009 10-K originally filed 2010-03-01)
form=8-K filing_date=2011-01-25, shortly followed by CORRESP 2011-01-26 and UPLOAD 2011-02-23, just before cutoff date 2011-02-28

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Annual data available for Revenues, NetIncomeLoss, and cash flow items spanning 2007-2009 (e.g., Revenues=7832062000 (2007), Revenues=9600564000 (2008), Revenues=8826933000 (2009)) plus quarterly data through Q3 2010

FY2010 10-K not yet filed as of cutoff 2011-02-28, limiting full-year 2010 comparison; some line items (e.g., ProceedsFromIssuanceOfLongTermDebt, NetCashProvidedByUsedInFinancingActivities) lack full 2010 annual figures

affected_line_items: Previously filed 10-K (FY2009) and 10-Q (Q2 2009, Q2 2010) financial statements
mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Restatement/correction of previously issued financial statements: a 10-K/A was filed 2010-04-30 amending the FY2009 Form 10-K (originally filed 2010-03-01), and two separate 10-Q/A amendments were filed (2009-09-01 for Q2 2009; 2010-09-01 for Q2 2010). This pattern of repeated post-issuance corrections, combined with a marked increase in SEC UPLOAD/CORRESP correspondence through January-February 2011 and an 8-K filed 2011-01-25 just before the cutoff, indicates unresolved accounting issues that likely required correction of previously reported income, tax, or balance sheet amounts.

rationale_evidence.quote: form=8-K filing_date=2011-01-25 followed by CORRESP 2011-01-26 and UPLOAD 2011-02-23

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Insufficient impairment recognition on goodwill, intangibles, and PP&E during a period of revenue contraction and margin compression, allowing carrying values to keep growing (via acquisitions/capex) even as the underlying business shrank, potentially delaying required impairment charges.

rationale_evidence.quote: PropertyPlantAndEquipmentNet=5922172000 (2008-12-31) rising to PropertyPlantAndEquipmentNet=6991579000 (2009-12-31), an 18% increase
Revenues=9600564000 (2008) fell to Revenues=8826933000 (2009), an 8.1% decline, while CostOfRevenue=8123078000 (2009) implies gross margin compression from ~20.6% (2008) to ~8.0% (2009)

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Potential aggressive revenue recognition or extended customer credit terms causing receivables to grow even as top-line revenue declined, inflating reported revenue quality or delaying recognition of collection risk.

rationale_evidence.quote: ReceivablesNetCurrent=2442848000 (2008-12-31) rising to ReceivablesNetCurrent=2504876000 (2009-12-31), a 2.5% increase while Revenues fell 8.1% over the same period
rationale_evidence.location: 10-K FY2009 balance sheet and income statement

top_signals: Two 10-Q/A amendments (Q2 2009, Q2 2010) and one 10-K/A (FY2009) filed within the observation window, indicating corrections to previously issued financial statements
Sharp escalation in SEC UPLOAD (comment letter) and CORRESP correspondence clustered from mid-2010 through February 2011, alongside an 8-K filed 2011-01-25 shortly before the cutoff

Goodwill, intangibles, PP&E, and receivables all grew materially in 2009 even as revenue declined 8.1% year-over-year, suggesting possible under-recognition of impairments or aggressive asset/receivable carrying values
Persistent gap between NetIncomeLoss and operating cash flow in 2007-2008 (NI exceeding OCF by ~18-21%), a classic accrual-quality warning sign
